Graded Novelty Encoding Task - art narrative analysis

The paradigm

This is the GNET paradigm with an added narrative to improve immersion of the participants.

Participants

The online task has been started a total of 118 times.

We excluded a total of 2 runs due to irregular data leaving 116 in the analysis.

We excluded some (1) runs as they missed more than 66 of 175 trials.

We also excluded 10 runs in which the participant produced less than 0.3 corrected recognition rate for familiars in the study phase.

We also excluded 16 participants as they overused “maybe” responses in the test phase (more than 25).

This left a total of 84 participants.

Maybe responses [mean(±sd)] : 9.9438202 ± 9.2088884

The sample had a mean(±sd) age of 19.7738095 ± 1.6232586.

The sample contained 67 women.

## corrplot 0.92 loaded

Study phase:

During the study phase, the participants see familiar and novel items.

The participants produced a mean (±sd) recognition rate of 0.687619 ± 0.1588484 for familiar pictures.

The participants produced a mean (±sd) corrected recognition rate of 0.5740476 ± 0.1703796 for familiar pictures.

Uncorrected: The sample performed above chance levels, as a one sample t-test showed (t(83)=10.8251445, p = 7.6778543^{-18}, Cohen’s d = 1.1811201, 95% CI:0.7107705 - 1.6514697).

Corrected: The sample performed above chance levels, as a one sample t-test showed (t(83)=30.8794823, p = 1.421084^{-47}, Cohen’s d = 3.3692277, 95% CI:2.6941856 - 4.0442699).

We use an ANOVA to see if there is a difference in correct rejection for the different novels.

##  quartic      2.274 1.297 83   1.754  0.0832

Test phase:

This phase tests the learning that has occurred during the study phase. If the participants learned the novels in the study phase, their responses should differ for the stimuli that they have already seen during the study phase (familiars) and those that they see for the first time during the test phase (novels). We also let the participants to choose “maybe” by pressing the down arrow, but asked them not to overuse this response (maybe resp <= 50). We added these responses as correct responses to both stimulus categories.

Maybe responses (MEAN ± SD): 10.2380952 ± 9.36363.

## 
## Attaching package: 'rstatix'
## The following object is masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter
## [1] "A t-test to compare the number of old responses btwn old and new stim"

Results of the paired t-test: (t(419)=2.1717451, p = 0.0304352, Cohen’s d = 0.098617, 95% CI:0.0092716 - 0.1879624).

Shapiro-Wilk is significant (p = 7.3416954^{-7}).

We used a Wilcoxon sgined-rank test. Z = -1.8971113, p = 0.0289066

ANOVA - Corrected Recog

ANOVA (factor: TYPE (5 lvls)): (F[3.812289,316.4199863] = 3.1231082, p = 0.0169159, n2G = 0.0267188).

Mixed models

The metrics

-DTF - Difference To corresponding Familiar picture All pictures are compared to a familiar. The number of differences between the three vectors of the pictures yields a difference score.

-DTAF - mean Difference To All Familiars We calculate the difference from all prototypes and take the mean for each picture in the set.

-DTN - mean Difference To all other Novels in the same category We calculate the differences between each pair of pictures and then take the mean of these differences (19 comparisons per stim).

Study phase

With these models i tried to assess which difference index explains familiar v novel distinction in study phase the best. As far as i understand, the model with the smallest AIC fits best to our data, which means that the mean difference from the corresponding familiar worked best here. The models all show, that the more different pictures are categorized with the highest efficacy.

## Generalized linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood (Laplace
##   Approximation) [glmerMod]
##  Family: binomial  ( logit )
## Formula: st_corr ~ DTF + (1 | multichar_knowresp)

Test phase

Models #1 - without random slope term

Logistic mixed regression for the test phase. Does difference from prototypes or familiarity/novelty have an effect on correct categorization in the test phase?

These models use trial quality (familiar / novel) as a fixed and as a random effect.

rt_resp is recognition test response, 0 stands for ‘old’ and 1 stands for ‘new’.

## Generalized linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood (Laplace
##   Approximation) [glmerMod]

Models #2 - with random slope (trial quality)

Logistic mixed regression for the test phase. Does difference from prototypes or familiarity/novelty have an effect on correct categorization in the test phase?

These models use trial quality (familiar / novel) as a fixed effect only.

rt_resp is recognition test response, 0 stands for ‘old’ and 1 stands for ‘new’.
